CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Mar-13-2024 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Jan-18-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard in Department 301 - Asbestos/CEQA Department.

Appearances:
Steven J. Patti of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing in person for plaintiff;
Mariam Pambukyan of WFBM, LLP (714-634-2522), appearing via CourtCall for defendants General Electric Company and Paramount Global;
Robert J. Bugatto of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant foster Wheeler.

Ruling:
Not contested, not argued, and tentative ruling adopted as follows: Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). No opposition filed.

1. The trial date is May 6, 2024 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206.
a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is May 17, 2024.
b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20 except as noted below.
2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is April 30, 2024.
a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice under the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise.
b. The Court will allow eight summary judgment/adjudication motions to be calendared in Asbestos Law and Motion for this action, unless the Court finds good cause for additional summary judgment/adjudication motions to be filed.
c. Before a party files and serves such summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation.
3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days.
a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier.
b. If there is any kind of dispute requiring meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone.
4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.
5. The fact discovery cut-off date is April 19, 2024.
6. The expert discovery cut-off date is May 3, 2024.
7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than February 20, 2024.
a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants of this no later than five days after submission.
b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(mini minutes continues in part 2 of 2.)

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR TENTATIVE RULING ENTRY PURPOSES ONLY)
Off Calendar Jan-18-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: (Part 2 of 2)
(Tentative ruling continued from previous entry)

8. No later than February 6, 2024:
a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant.
b. Defendants must also provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of Defendant's affirmative defenses.
c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown.
9. A further case management conference is set for March 19, 2024 at 1:30 p.m.
a. There is no need for the parties to file case management conference statements.
b. The parties may appear at the conference by telephone.
c. If the parties agree that no CMC is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two days prior to take the CMC off calendar.
10. To schedule a mandatory settlement conference, all counsel and self-represented litigants must jointly e-mail the ADR Coordinator for the San Francisco Superior Court at ADRCoordinator@sftc.org. The email must contain the case name and number, a brief description of the case, and a date for the settlement conference that is no later than April 29, 2024.
a. Plaintiffs will make a settlement demand on Defendants no later than two court days before the MSC. All parties will provide confidential settlement demands/offers representing their best and final settlement offers to the assigned settlement judge one court day before the MSC. These settlement demands/offers will be sent to the settlement judge electronically at an address provided by the ADR coordinator. Order signed in open court. Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Not present. Not reported. (301/RCE)
